name: Barley (food medium)
basis: per 100 g whole grain (hulled/dehulled barley kernel, ~10% moisture as-is)

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 170283 (by difference: carbohydrate 73.5 - fiber 17.3 - sugars 0.8); Henry 1988 doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1988.tb04560.x
components.evidence: Amylose+amylopectin are the overwhelmingly dominant carbon/energy source of the grain (~52-58 g/100g); the true growth substrate for amylolytic microbes.

components.name: total dietary fiber
components.evidence: Non-starch polysaccharide fraction; includes beta-glucan, arabinoxylan, cellulose.

components.name: (1,3)(1,4)-beta-D-glucan
components.source: Frontiers Sustain Food Syst 2023 doi:10.3389/fsufs.2023.1326716 (mean 4.55-4.80); PMC6963557
components.evidence: Signature soluble mixed-linkage glucan of barley (range ~2.5-11%); major fermentable polysaccharide for gut/soil microbiota.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Principal free (non-reducing) sugar of mature barley grain (~1.2% DM, declining from 1.8% during maturation).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Reducing sugar; abundant in developing grain (~2% DM) but only trace in mature kernels.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Reducing sugar; ~3% DM in developing grain, trace in mature grain.

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence: Alpha-galactoside; ~0.4% DM in mature kernels (peaks ~0.7% at 36 DAA).

components.name: fructans (fructo-oligosaccharides / graminan)
components.evidence: Glucodifructose/graminan-series fructosans (range ~0.6-2.5%); readily fermentable oligosaccharides largely excluded from USDA 'total sugars'.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Kim/Peukert GC-MS PMC5302243; MDPI Crops 2024 4(4):38 doi:10.3390/crops4040038
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid of barley grain and highest among wheat/barley/oat/rye; entirely absent from the prior FooDB profile. Absolute mg/100g not quantified in accessible sources.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Second most abundant TCA organic acid in barley grain after malate; detected as its methyl ester in GC-MS. Not absolutely quantified.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Among the most abundant organic acids detected across cereals; present in barley grain. Not absolutely quantified.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: TCA-cycle acid present in barley grain (lower than malate/citrate). Not absolutely quantified.

components.name: alpha-ketoglutaric acid
components.evidence: 2-oxoglutarate; reported HIGHEST in barley among the four cereals compared. Not absolutely quantified.

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: Bhatt 2021 (Indian barley) ResearchGate 357614244 (mean 7.01 mg/g, range 3.85-9.85); Dai 2007 doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2007.03.063
components.evidence: Dominant storage form of phosphorus (~0.4-1.0 g/100g); strong chelator that lowers bioavailable Fe/Zn/Ca/Mg/P for microbes.

components.name: gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA)
components.evidence: Free non-protein amino acid; usable N source.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Hordeins/glutelins/albumins; major bound-amino-acid nitrogen source (amino acid profile below).

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-derived (total) amino acid profile.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First-limiting amino acid of barley protein.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid, protein-derived.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid (as cystine), protein-derived.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid (was MISSING from prior profile).

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Aspartic acid, protein-derived.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Glutamic acid; most abundant amino acid (hordein-rich).

components.name: glycine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Very high in barley (proline-rich hordeins).

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid (was MISSING from prior profile).

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Germ-associated lipid; predominantly polyunsaturated.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of barley oil (~50% of total FA).

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Principal monounsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Main saturated fatty acid.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: n-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Macromineral.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Largely phytate-bound.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Macromineral cofactor.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: Mostly stored as phytate (see phytic acid).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: Low native sodium.

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: Trace mineral (phytate-chelated).

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Trace mineral.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Trace mineral.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Trace mineral (soil-dependent).

components.name: boron
components.evidence: Genuine plant micronutrient present at trace levels; retained (moved from uncovered).

components.name: silicon
components.evidence: Genuine; barley is a silica-accumulating grass. Retained (moved from uncovered).

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: B-vitamin cofactor.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: B-vitamin cofactor.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: B-vitamin (largely bound as niacytin in cereals).

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.evidence: B-vitamin cofactor (CoA precursor).

components.name: vitamin B6 (pyridoxine)
components.evidence: B-vitamin cofactor.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: B-vitamin cofactor.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present in barley grain; not quantified in USDA panel.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Quaternary amine; methyl donor / membrane precursor.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Lipid-soluble antioxidant (barley also rich in tocotrienols).

components.name: vitamin K (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Phytomenadione.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Trace provitamin-A carotenoid; barley grain is essentially carotenoid-poor.

components.name: vitamin C (L-ascorbate)
components.evidence: Effectively absent in dry mature grain; retained at ~0 (prior FooDB entry overstated).

components.name: ferulic acid
components.source: Idehen 2017 review; PMC7346127; colored-barley study doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2018.05.100 (45-1027 µg/g)
components.evidence: Dominant phenolic acid (~68% of total phenolic acids), largely ester-bound to arabinoxylan in the cell wall; midpoint estimate, wide range.

components.name: hordenine
components.evidence: Signature barley N-methyl-tyramine-derived alkaloid; genuine, retained.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine compatible solute accumulated by barley; usable methyl/N source. Retained.

components.name: putrescine
components.evidence: Genuine grain polyamine; retained at trace level.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion; ultratrace FooDB artifact, barley grain has negligible cobalt/B12

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal metabolite, biologically implausible in a plant grain; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite, implausible in a plant grain; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plants supply carotenoids, not retinol; FooDB artifact

remove.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
remove.reason: oxidation artifact of methionine, not a genuine nutrient pool

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: soy isoflavone; not characteristic of barley; FooDB predicted/implausible

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: legume-type isoflavones not characteristic of barley; FooDB predicted

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin typical of Apiaceae/Rutaceae; implausible in barley; FooDB predicted

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial fermentation product / food additive, not a barley grain constituent

remove.name: Dimethylamine
remove.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

remove.name: Methylamine
remove.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

remove.name: Diethylamine
remove.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

remove.name: Ethanamine
remove.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

remove.name: Piperidine
remove.reason: trace volatile amine, non-nutritive FooDB noise

missing_added: malic acid (dominant organic acid) + citric, succinic, fumaric, alpha-ketoglutaric acids (organic-acid class entirely absent before)
starch (~55 g/100g) and total dietary fiber (17.3 g) as the true dominant carbon sources
specific free sugars with amounts: sucrose (principal), glucose, fructose, raffinose, fructans
L-valine and L-serine (missing amino acids) + quantitative amounts for the full protein-derived amino-acid profile
quantitative minerals (Ca, Fe, Mg, P, K, Na, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se) and vitamins (B1,B2,B3,B5,B6,B9,E,K, choline)
keep_uncovered: Catechin, Procyanidin B3, Prodelphinidin B3 (genuine barley flavan-3-ols / proanthocyanidins)
N-Methyltyramine, Dopamine, Serotonin, Histamine, Trigonelline (genuine barley biogenic amines/alkaloids at trace)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170283 (Barley, hulled, SR Legacy) — macronutrients, sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
MacLeod 1954 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1954.tb02747.x; MacLeod 1953 doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1953.tb06210.x — free sugars & fructosans
LaBerge 1973 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1973.tb03568.x — free-sugar changes during maturation
Henry 1988 J Inst Brew doi:10.1002/j.2050-0416.1988.tb04560.x — barley carbohydrate review
Kim/Peukert et al. GC-MS metabolomic profiling of wheat/barley/oat/rye PMC5302243 — organic acids (malate, alpha-ketoglutarate highest in barley)
MDPI Crops 2024 4(4):38 doi:10.3390/crops4040038 — pre/post-milling barley organic & amino acid profiling
Bhatt 2021 (Indian barley phytic acid) ResearchGate 357614244; Dai 2007 doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2007.03.063 — phytic acid
Frontiers Sustain Food Syst 2023 doi:10.3389/fsufs.2023.1326716; PMC6963557 — beta-glucan
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~55 g/100g), with beta-glucan (~4.5 g) and total fiber (17.3 g) as major fermentable polysaccharides; free sugars are minor and led by sucrose (~0.6 g). The organic-acid class (malic dominant, then citric/succinic/fumaric/alpha-ketoglutarate) was entirely missing from the FooDB profile and is the key addition, though authoritative absolute mg/100g values are not published for raw grain (added with null amounts + rank notes). Nitrogen is chiefly protein-bound (12.5 g, glutamate/proline-rich hordeins). Removed animal metabolites (creatine, taurine, retinol), toxic/ultratrace metals (arsenate, mercury, nickel, aluminium, tin, free cobalt), an oxidation artifact (methionine sulfoxide), implausible non-barley phytochemicals (genistein/isoflavones, oxypeucedanin, epsilon-polylysine), and trace volatile amines.
